purpose: Set organisational guardrails before choosing workload services.
subcategories:
Cloud Foundation & Governance
FinOps, Cost & Sustainability
Cloud Operations & Management
Architecture Assurance & Service Health
primaryDecisionQuestion: What operating model, account hierarchy, policy, budget and ownership model will govern the solution?
typicalSolutionEngineeringOutputs: Landing zone, tagging, policy baseline, budget, RACI and architecture principles.
stopRule: Do not select workload services before ownership, policy, tagging and budget are clear.

purpose: Establish identity, security, privacy and compliance controls.
subcategories:
Identity & Access
Cryptography, Keys & Secrets
Network & Application Security
Data Protection & Privacy
Threat Detection & Security Operations
Governance, Posture & Compliance
primaryDecisionQuestion: Who can access what, how is data protected, and how will threats and evidence be managed?
typicalSolutionEngineeringOutputs: Identity model, threat model, control matrix, logging plan and compliance evidence.
stopRule: Do not treat a product name as a security design; define identities, threats and evidence.

purpose: Design boundaries, connectivity, routing and delivery paths.
subcategories:
Virtual Networks & Segmentation
Traffic Management & Content Delivery
DNS & Naming
Hybrid & Private Connectivity
Service Networking & Private Access
Network Operations
primaryDecisionQuestion: Where does traffic flow, which paths are private, and what resilience and latency are required?
typicalSolutionEngineeringOutputs: Network topology, IP plan, ingress/egress design, DNS and connectivity pattern.
stopRule: Avoid unnecessary public paths and duplicated network appliances.

purpose: Choose the execution model with the least necessary operational burden.
subcategories:
Virtual Machines & Core Compute
Serverless Compute
Containers & Kubernetes
Managed Application Platforms
Specialised & High-Performance Compute
Hybrid, Multicloud & Edge Runtime
primaryDecisionQuestion: How much control is truly required versus managed simplicity?
typicalSolutionEngineeringOutputs: Runtime decision, scaling model, availability design and operations ownership.
stopRule: Use the least operationally complex runtime that meets the requirement.

purpose: Select storage and operational databases from access patterns.
subcategories:
Object & Archive Storage
Block & Local Storage
File & High-Performance Storage
Relational Databases
Distributed & Cloud-Native SQL
NoSQL Databases
Caching & In-Memory Data
Specialised Databases
Backup & Disaster Recovery
Data Transfer & Hybrid Storage
primaryDecisionQuestion: What data model, consistency, latency, durability, retention and recovery requirements apply?
typicalSolutionEngineeringOutputs: Data-store selection, backup/DR, migration and lifecycle policy.
stopRule: Choose from access patterns and recovery needs—not database popularity.

purpose: Build governed pipelines from raw data to business insight.
subcategories:
Data Lake, Warehouse & Lakehouse
Data Engineering & Orchestration
Streaming & Real-Time Data
Data Governance, Sharing & Quality
Analytics, BI & Search
primaryDecisionQuestion: How will data be ingested, governed, transformed, served and consumed?
typicalSolutionEngineeringOutputs: Data architecture, ingestion pattern, semantic model, governance and BI platform.
stopRule: Avoid duplicating warehouse, lakehouse and BI responsibilities.

purpose: Select AI capabilities only after data, risk and evaluation requirements are clear.
subcategories:
Foundation Models & GenAI
Agents, RAG & Enterprise Search
ML Platform & MLOps
Responsible AI, Safety & Governance
Prebuilt Cognitive AI Services
Industry & Applied AI
primaryDecisionQuestion: What intelligence is needed, what evidence proves quality, and what risks require controls?
typicalSolutionEngineeringOutputs: Model/platform decision, RAG/agent design, evaluation plan and AI control framework.
stopRule: Do not scale an AI prototype without evaluation, governance and operating controls.

purpose: Compose user experiences, APIs, events and enterprise workflows.
subcategories:
Messaging & Eventing
Workflow & Orchestration
Enterprise Integration & iPaaS
Application Communications
End-User, Collaboration & Contact Centre
primaryDecisionQuestion: How will users and systems interact, integrate and exchange data reliably?
typicalSolutionEngineeringOutputs: Application architecture, API contract, integration pattern and channel design.
stopRule: Choose the integration semantic first: request, event, queue, stream or workflow.

purpose: Create repeatable delivery, observability and change mechanisms.
subcategories:
Software Delivery & Supply Chain
Developer Experience & Platform Engineering
Testing & Resilience Engineering
Observability & Application Performance
Reliability, SRE & Service Health
Migration, Modernisation & Transformation
primaryDecisionQuestion: How will the solution be built, released, observed, supported and evolved?
typicalSolutionEngineeringOutputs: CI/CD, golden path, SLOs, runbooks, migration plan and support model.
stopRule: No production approval without CI/CD, observability, runbooks and support ownership.

purpose: Use specialist capabilities only where the industry requirement justifies them.
subcategories:
IoT, Devices & Industrial Edge
Media, Communications & Location
Industry & Sovereign Solutions
primaryDecisionQuestion: Which physical, edge, media, sovereign or industry constraints cannot be met by general services?
typicalSolutionEngineeringOutputs: Edge topology, device model, specialist service selection and industry controls.
stopRule: Use specialist services only when a real edge, media, sovereign or industry constraint exists.
